# 

# Approximate Pricing {#approximate-pricing}

| Common | 100 gp |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Uncommon** | 400 gp |
| **Rare** | 4000 gp |
| **Very Rare** | 40,000 gp |
| **Legendary** | 200,000 gp |
| **Artifact** | Priceless |

Note: Consumable items are half of the typical cost for their rarity.

# 

# Mundane Items {#mundane-items}

---

**Anarchic Water**  
*Adventuring gear*  
25 gp, 1 lb.

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Anarchic Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take 2d8 damage if it is a Construct. Roll a d8 for the damage type: 1\. Acid; 2\. Cold; 3\. Fire; 4\. Force; 5\. Lightning; 6\. Poison; 7\. Psychic; 8\. Thunder.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Axe**  
*Adventuring gear*  
*2 gp, 4 lb.*

Working for 10 minutes, you can use an Axe to cut down a tree less than 5ft in diameter. Working for 1 hour, you can use an Axe to dig a hole that is 5 feet on each side in wood or similar material.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Axiomatic Water**  
*Adventuring gear*  
25 gp, 1 lb.

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Axiomatic Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take 2d8 Force damage if it is an Aberration or an Elemental.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Crab Suit**  
*Adventuring Gear, Exotic*

*To create a cheaper, more easily produced version of the popular Fish Suit, gnomish inventor Nadul DaRoni created the Crab Suit, a suit of armor made to handle deep water pressure.*

This bulky suit, which fully encases your head and body, takes 10 minutes to don or doff. While worn, it enables you to breathe in an airless environment and renders you immune to the harmful effects of any gas that surrounds you for up to 8 hours. 

Once worn for 8 total hours, the suit's property is unusable unless the suit is recharged for 1 hour in an elemental air node (or dedicated Crab Suit Recharger).

The Crab Suit allows the user to withstand depth pressures of up to 750m, with more resistant models able to withstand even greater pressure.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Darkvision Powder**  
*Adventuring gear*  
*30gp, 1 lb.*

This plain gray powder clings to surfaces and cannot be seen with normal vision from more than 10 feet away. It glows brightly when viewed with darkvision, and thus is useful for creatures that have darkvision and wish to write messages that other creatures can’t read. Each vial contains enough powder for a message of up to 25 words. By adding a mixture of spider silk oil, an ink can be created with the same properties as the powder.

*This item can be crafted using Alchemy Tools.*

*From S3, 3.5e Drow of the Underdark, ported by Aris*  
---

**Diving Mask**  
*Adventuring Gear, Exotic*

Comprised of an airtight mask, goggles, and an air tank, the Diving Mask was made as a portable solution to air deprivation and light underwater travel.

The Diving Mask allows the user to connect to an air tank, expanding their breathing capacity and prolonging suffocation. A full air tank connected to a diving mask provides 6 hours' worth of breathable air. 

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Faeorran Wine (Bottle)**  
*Food and Drink, Exotic*  
*10 gp*

*This wine is made from Sweet Faeorra, a rare grape-like fruit found in the feywild. The wine is a deep red, very sweet, and very high in alcohol content.*

A creature that drinks an excessive amount of this wine must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it becomes Poisoned for 1 minute. 

Whenever it ends its turn while Poisoned in this way it moves 10 feet in a random movement. Roll 1d4 for the direction: 1, north; 2, east; 3, south; or 4, west. If it collides with an object or creature within this movement, it falls Prone. This movement provokes Opportunity Attacks.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Gummy Apple**  
*Food and Drinks, Exotic*

Mmmmmm Yummy appl. Eating this is food. Yumm

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Messenger Pigeon**  
*Familiar, Adventuring Gear (Animal)*  
*25gp (Trained), 15gp (Untrained, Requires Bastion Turn)*  
*![][image1]*

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Paxel**  
*Adventuring Gear, Exotic*  
5 lb.

The Paxel is a tool that combines the utility of a shovel, an axe, and a pickaxe. Working for 1 hour, you can use the Paxel to displace a 5ft Cube of soil, stone, or wood.

*From WM3, Made by Jarod*  
---

**Pickaxe**  
Adventuring Gear  
2 gp, 10 lb.

Working for 1 hour, you can use a Pickaxe to mine a hole that is 5 feet on each side in soft rock, or 8 hours for hard rock. Using a Pickaxe gives you Advantage on Strength checks to remove gems and minerals from stone.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Sheet Music**  
*Mundane, Adventuring Gear, Exotic*

*This piece of parchment teaches the reader how to play a specific written piece of music, so long as the user knows the notation.*

When you attempt to play the song written on this piece of sheet music and you are Proficient with the instrument you are playing, any roll of 4 or lower on the D20 roll is treated as a 5 instead. Any given piece of sheet music must depict a specific song, and its benefits only apply to playing the given song.

This item may be copied and printed at a scriptorium.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Stiletto-heeled Boots**  
*Adventuring gear*  
10 gp, 1 lb

These boots can be wielded as a dagger while on your feet. However, they have a \-2 to hit, unless the boot is wielded in a hand, you are airborne, or the attack is made against a Prone creature.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Swim Clothes**  
*Adventuring gear*  
1 gp, 0.5 lb.

These clothes are treated to be water resistant, quick drying, stretchy, and low absorption, great for swimming in\!

*Made by Landon*  
---

**Teeth Bomb**  
*Explosive*  
*200 GP, 1 lb., Exotic*

As an Utilize action, you can light a Bomb and throw it at a point up to 60 feet away, where it explodes. Each creature in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point makes a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d6 Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

*Made by Marvin*  
---

**Thinker's Tools**  
*Artisan's Tools, Exotic*  
*50gp, 1 lb.*

**Ability:** Intelligence  
**Utilize:** Ponder (DC 20\)  
**Craft:** Book (Fiction)

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Unholy Water**  
*Adventuring Gear*  
25 gp, 1 lb.

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Unholy Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take 2d8 Necrotic damage if it is a Celestial or a Plant.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Urchin Venom**  
*Adventuring gear, poison (injury), Exotic*  
25 gp

A creature subjected to Urchin Venom must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute.

*Made by Aris, Adapted from 3.5e There Is No Honor*   
---

**Vrock Dust**  
*Drug*  
*40gp per dose*

This powder made from the dried spores of a vrock provides a potent, pleasurable high when inhaled. A creature that consumes this drug must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature is Poisoned for the next 10 minutes. While Poisoned in this way, the creature is Vulnerable to Radiant damage and automatically fails any saving throw inflicted by a Celestial or Fiend. 

A creature addicted to Vrock Dust experiences potent cravings for more Vrock Dust, and will often do anything it can to obtain additional doses of the drug.

*Credit to FungusAmongUs's Abi-Dalzim’s Abhorrent Adventurers*

*Repassed from WM3*  
---

## Additional Instruments {#additional-instruments}

---

**Performance Prop**  
*Instrument*  
30 gp, 1 lb  
Ability: Charisma  
Utilize: Put on a rehearsed show (DC 10), or improvise a show (DC 15\)  
---

**Megaphone**  
Instrument  
3 gp, 1 lb  
Ability: Charisma  
Utilize: Sing a known tune (DC 10), or improvise a song (DC 15\)  
---

**Oration Book**  
*Instrument*  
*25 gp, 5 lbs*  
Ability: Charisma  
Utilize: Put on a rehearsed performance (DC 10), or improvise a performance (DC 15\)

## New Weapons {#new-weapons}

---

**Juggling Club**  
*Simple ranged weapon*  
*1d4 Bludgeoning*  
*Finesse, Light, Thrown (20/60 ft.)*  
*Mastery: Nick*  
*1 gp, 1/4lb.*

*Made by Gem*

# 

# Magic Items {#magic-items}

## Common Items {#common-items}

---

**\+1 Tool**  
*Generic Variant (Tool or Artisan Tool), Common*

You gain a \+1 bonus to ability checks made using this tool.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Abjurer's Necklace**  
*Wondrous Item, Common (Requires Attunement by a Wizard)*

This enchanted amulet is popular among the wizards of Waterdeep. You can use the amulet to cast the shield spell. Once cast, the amulet can't be used again until the next dawn.

*From S3 and Adapted from Warriors of Waterdeep by Aris*  
---

**Adamantine Axe**  
Wondrous Item (Axe), Common

An Adamantine Axe is twice as efficient as a regular axe, being able to cut down trees up to 10ft in diameter in 10 minutes, and clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Adamantine Paxel**  
*Wondrous Item (Paxel), Common, Exotic*

An Adamantine Paxel is twice as efficient as a regular Paxel, clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Adamantine Pickaxe**  
*Wondrous Item (Pickaxe), Common*

An Adamantine Pickaxe is twice as efficient as a regular pickaxe, clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Adamantine Shovel**  
*Wondrous Item (Shovel), Common*

An Adamantine Shovel is twice as efficient as a regular shovel, clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Appraiser's Glass**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

The Appraiser's Glass functions as a Magnifying Glass. As a Magic action while holding the glass, you can spend cast Approximate.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Barbecue Hammers**  
*Wondrous Item, Common, Consumable*  
*12 lbs.*

Made with love... and *hammers*.

As a Utilize Action, you can eat it.  When you consume the hammers, you must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you take 2d4 Bludgeoning damage.

The hammers provide as much nourishment as 1 day of Rations

*Made by Marvin*  
---

**Beast Mode Indicator Vestments**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This shirt reads “Beast Mode: Off”. As you enter a temporarily heightened state, such as a Rage or a physical transformation, you may choose to rip this shirt off to reveal another shirt underneath that reads “Beast Mode: On”. Once this state ends, the destroyed shirt reappears on you, undamaged.

*Made by V*  
---

**Boots of a Zephyr**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

*\+1 Aura. These babies have NO mechanical benefit.*

While you wear these boots, you can take a Magic action to appear to float 4 inches above a surface. This effect lasts until you take another Magic action to end it.

*Made by Althaea*  
---

**Boots of Orcish Kind**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

While you wear these boots, your steps make a bunch of sound, regardless of the surface you are moving across. You also have Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Boots of Stomping**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

While you wear these boots, you can pass through the space of Prone creatures as if they were Incapacitated. When you pass through the space of a Prone creature, you can choose to deal 1d4 Bludgeoning damage to it. A creature takes this damage no more than once per turn.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Bottle of Boundless Kaeth**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This metal bottle carries delicious, warm kaeth. The bottle comes with a stopper, which is attached to the bottle by a little chain. Even when open, the bottle won't accept any liquid other than the kaeth it produces. The kaeth inside is always comfortably warm, and none of the heat can be felt through the bottle.

Each time you drink the kaeth, roll a d20. On a 1, the bottle refuses to dispense kaeth for the next hour. If you pour kaeth from the bottle, rather than drinking from it, the kaeth vanishes the moment it leaves the bottle.

*From Strixhaven: A Curriculum of Chaos*  
---

**Cheese With Nothin’**  
*Wondrous Item, Common, Consumable*

*“Nothin’?”*

Sometimes erroneously referred to as a simple stick of cheese, this pizza with extra cheese has no dough or sauce. Despite being a very simple pizza, it serves as a day’s rations for up to 8 people.

*Made by Miel*  
---

**Cinnabar Eye Cusps**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

These are pink lenses of polarized crystal that fit comfortably over your eyes. When worn in an area with an intense concentration of faerzress, the eye cusps enhance your vision. If you lack darkvision, you gain darkvision out to 120 feet. If you have darkvision, your darkvision extends through the entire faerzress-infused area.

*From 3.5e Drow of the Underdark, ported by Aris*  
---

**Coin of Delving**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This scintillating copper coin sheds dim light in a 5-foot Emanation. If dropped a distance greater than 5 feet, the coin issues a melodious ringing sound when it hits a surface. Any creature that can hear the chime can determine the distance the coin dropped based on the tone.

*From Explorer’s Guide to Wildemount*  
---

**Deck of Dusk**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

*Created in the image of the Deck of Some Things, this deck of ivory or vellum cards bestows an assortment of minor benefits and penalties on those who draw from it. Most (75 percent) of these decks have only thirteen cards, but the rest have twenty-two.*

*Before you draw a card, you must declare how many cards you intend to draw, then draw them randomly. Any additional cards drawn have no effect. Unless a card states otherwise, as soon as you draw a card from the deck, its magic takes effect. You must draw each card you declared no more than 1 hour after the previous draw. If you fail to draw the chosen number, the remaining number of cards fly from the deck and take effect simultaneously.*

*Unless it is the Mystery card, a drawn card immediately takes effect, fades from existence, and reappears in the deck, making it possible to draw the same card multiple times.*

\`\`\`  
A ♦              | Dusk\*  
K ♦              | Dusk  
Q ♦              | Dusk  
J ♦              | Dusk  
2 ♦              | Dusk\*  
A ♥              | Dusk\*  
K ♥              | Dusk  
Q ♥              | Dusk  
J ♥              | Dusk  
2 ♥              | Dusk\*  
A ♣              | Dusk\*  
K ♣              | Dusk  
Q ♣              | Dusk  
J ♣              | Dusk  
2 ♣              | Dusk\*  
A ♠              | Dusk\*  
K ♠              | Dusk  
Q ♠              | Dusk  
J ♠              | Dusk  
2 ♠              | Dusk\*  
Joker (with™)    | Dusk\*  
Joker (without™) | Dusk  
\`\`\`

**Dusk.** This card supernaturally saps your energy. You have Disadvantage on Initiative rolls. This effect lasts until you finish a Long Rest, but it can be ended early by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Destination Orb**  
*Wondrous Item, Common, Consumable*

Destination Orbs are engraved with the sigil sequence of a Teleportation Circle. A creature who holds it immediately learns the sequence. Whenever a creature holding the orb casts Teleportation Circle targeting the sigil sequence engraved in the orb, it can choose to crush the orb instead of expending a spell slot.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Doublet of Dramatic Demise**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This striking satin jacket allows you to make the most of your dying moments. When you are Unconscious with 0 Hit Points while wearing this doublet, you can speak and communicate.

You make Death Saving Throws as normal, but if you roll a 1 on the d20, you swoon with a loud gasp and die instantly.

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Ek-Sel Slate**  
*Wondrous Item, Common, Unique*

A small clay tablet. There are rows intersecting columns, with cuneiform written in some of the boxes. It is a tabular account of livestock and feed.

When holding this tablet, you feel the intelligence and logic of the long-lost denizens of Ek-Sel flow through you, and you gain advantage on checks towards solving mathematical problems and equations.

*Made by Sam*  
---

**Everburning Torch**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

When this torch is ignited, it cannot be extinguished unless a creature within 5 feet of the torch uses a Magic action to do so.

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Fragile Weapon**  
*Weapon (Any), Common*

*This weapon is barely able to hold itself together, falling apart if the wrong person holds it. Pretty much every weapon in the world is one of these.*

Whenever a creature wielding one of these dies, the weapon immediately breaks apart and turns to dust.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Frostine Weapon**  
*Weapon (Any), Common, Exotic*

*This weapon is made from Frostine crystals, a cream colored gemstone that is perpetually cold.*

This weapon is cold enough to keep the temperature within 5 feet of it at 32 degrees Fahrenheit (0 degrees Celsius). It deals Cold damage instead of its normal type.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Gizmo\!**  
*Wondrous Item (Common), Consumable, Exotic*

*A strange assortment of magic runes and metal latticework. It's been given a haphazard shell and a safety seal of approval.*

While holding a Gizmo\!, you can use it to cast either Prestidigitation or Thaumaturgy. After doing so, it falls apart.

*Made by Christopher*  
---

**Glasses of Transcription**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

These boxy reading glasses have a black stripe along the bottom of each lens. When worn, they attempt to caption speech and noises relevant to the wearer, causing letters to appear in the black box. Speech is written in the language it is spoken in, while sound effects are captioned in a language familiar to the reader. Muffled speech, overlapping noises, and other forms of interference may impair the Glasses' ability to subtitle the world.  
---

**Heroic Costume**  
*Wondrous Item (Costume), Common*

*With the popularity of Liar's Night continuing to grow across Toril, people have begun to create a variety of magical costumes. This costume was made to imbue confidence in the wearer, depicting a famous adventurer or fantastical hero.*

While wearing this costume, you can use a Magic action to cast Heroism. When cast in this way, it has a range of Self and does not grant Temporary Hit Points. Once the spell is cast, it can't be cast from the costume again until the next sunset.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Hog of Cranking**  
*Wondrous Item, Common, Consumable*

*Bear Witness\!*

As a Magic action, you may begin cranking your hog. While cranking your hog, you cannot take reactions, as you are fully focused on cranking your hog. At the beginning of your next turn after beginning to crank your hog, 1 Boar appears in the space where the Hog of Cranking was (or within 5 ft. of that space if the space it occupied is currently occupied by another creature). You stop cranking your hog when the Boar is summoned, upon which the hog becomes non-magical. This boar is friendly to you and your allies and hostile to any creatures you may be hostile towards when you begin to crank your hog. The boar continues to fight said hostile creatures until it or the hostile creatures die or are knocked Unconscious. If no more hostile creatures remain, the boar acts as typical for a boar, and it no longer follows an obligation to be friendly towards you or your allies.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Lounge of Floating**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This hovering couch allows even the most injured of creatures to retain their mobility. While sitting, lying, reclining, or otherwise resting on this seat, you gain a 20 foot hovering speed from the lounge carrying you around. It cannot hover more than 5 feet above the ground or other solid surfaces, and if it exceeds this height, it plummets until it strikes a solid surface, dealing fall damage to the creature upon it. Only one creature of a size no greater than large can sit upon the lounge at a time. If more than one creature rests on the lounge, it sets itself on the ground as gently as it can. A single creature of up to tiny size, such as a pet cat or crab, can rest on the couch along with its primary occupant without causing it to cease hovering.

If more weight is placed on the Floating Lounge than the primary occupant's carrying capacity (not including the occupant themselves), it floats to the ground as gently as possible.

Paralysis caused by magic may, at the DM's discretion, also halt the lounge. Paralysis caused by physical injury, poison, or illness typically does not halt it.

The mental effort required to command the Floating Lounge is too great to permit resting while it hovers.

A surface held aloft by telekinesis, another creature, or most other magical means is not sufficiently solid for the lounge. (Please don't GMod physics this thing).  
---

**Mend-O**  
*Potion, Consumable, Common*  
*1/4 lb.*

*Developed on Mechanus, this vial of silvery liquid is able to repair anything it touches. Shaking the vial reveals it is full of many small flakes of metal.*

A vial of Mend-O can be applied to a tear in an object, or a damaged item. Applying Mend-O takes 1 minute, after which it magically seeps into whatever it is applied to, causing it to receive the benefits of a casting of Mending.

Alternatively, as a Bonus Action, you can drink it or administer it to another willing creature within 5 feet of yourself. If the creature that drinks the Mend-O is a Construct, it regains 2d4+2 Hit Points. If the creature is not a Construct, it must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 14 (4d6) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Message in a Bottle**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

When you cast the Message spell, you can choose to cast the spell into this bottle, provided it is empty and unsealed. When you speak the message, a scroll of paper with your message appears in the bottle, and a cork appears, sealing the bottle.

As a Utilize action, a creature can open this bottle while a message is contained within. That creature hears the stored message as if it was cast targeting them. Then, the message and cork disappear.

When found, there's a 75% chance a message is already stored in the bottle.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Mini-Mii**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

It's a little construct of a person. As a Magic action, a Construct can install this Mini-Mii inside of them. While a Construct has one installed, they have the personality of the Mini-Mii. A Construct can eject a Mini-Mii as a Magic action. If a Construct has multiple installed, their behavior might become....complicated. When not installed, it has the statistics of a Tiny Servant, though retaining its personality.

*Made by Landon*  
---

**Modron Earpiece**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

A bronze, clockwork earpiece with a rudimentary microphone attached. 

When speaking Common into the audio receptor, the earpiece converts the Common speech into Modron, emitting a sequence of clicks and whirs. When Modron is spoken and can be heard by the earpiece, the earpiece translates Modron into Common, emitting what is said but in Common into the ears of the wearer. 

Mechanically, Common speech of the wearer can be heard as Modron, and Modron spoken in the vicinity of the wearer can be heard as Common by the wearer.

*Made by Sam*  
---

**Mystery Potion (Common)**  
*Potion, Common, Consumable*

*Typically made as a byproduct by alchemists, this potion is fully unpredictable. Swirling this liquid causes its color to change rapidly between a variety of vibrant colors. Its smell is purely alchemical, providing no insight as to what could be inside.*

When you drink this potion, roll on the table below, gaining the effects of drinking or applying the rolled potion.

A creature can spend 1 hour studying the potion to attempt to identify and stabilize its effects. After the hour is done, the creature must make a DC 10 Intelligence (Alchemist's Supplies) check. On a pass, roll on the table transforming the Mystery Potion into the rolled potion.

\`\`\`  
1d4 | Potion Effect  
1   | Potion of Climbing  
2   | Potion of Comprehension  
3   | Potion of Healing  
4   | Potion of Watchful Rest  
\`\`\`

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Noble Weapon**  
*Weapon (Any), Common*

A weapon furnished with platinum and gold, giving it a regal presence. It shines brighter in the hands of those with great conviction.

**Glory Strike.** When you score a Critical Hit with this weapon, you deal an extra 1d6 Radiant damage. This extra damage is not doubled by the Critical Hit.

*Made by Christopher*  
---

**Ornate Costume**  
*Wondrous Item (Costume), Common*

*With the popularity of Liar's Night continuing to grow across Toril, people have begun to create a variety of magical costumes. This costume was made to impress others, sewn with fancy techniques and wealth.*

While wearing this costume, you can use a Magic action to cast Charm Person (Save DC 10). When cast in this way, it requires Concentration and has a duration of 1 minute. Once the spell is cast, it can't be cast from the costume again until the next sunset.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Painter’s Sun Hat**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This well-made hat can come in any shape, size, or form and from casual inspection seems to be mundane in nature. However, when the hat is placed on the ground as a Magic action and its command word is spoken, it begins to float into the air. The hovering hat then produces an illusion of a humanoid of your choice that you are familiar with, with the caveat that the illusion is wearing the hat. The figure can't move and fails to hold up to physical inspection. The illusion remains until the hat is moved out of place, or until the command word is spoken once more, causing the hat to float back to the ground. If a creature uses the Study action to examine the illusion, a DC 10 Intelligence (Investigation) check reveals the figure is an illusion.

*From Obojima: Tales from the Tall Grass*  
---

**Pig Snout**  
*Potion, Common, Consumable*

After drinking this pink pastel potion, there is a brief uncomfortable transformation, where the imbiber grows a beautiful, snuffling pig snout. For the next hour, you become very fond of truffles, perhaps even to the point of belligerence, and you have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

*From Obojima: Tales from the Tall Grass. Made by Chrys*  
---

**Plushie of Scent**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

A tiny, soft plushy that emits a particular scent.

This plushy emits a particular scent that can be perceived up to 10 feet away.  You can change the scent to a scent of your choice with a Magic action.

*Made by Marvin*  
---

**Plushie of Squeaking**   
*Wondrous Item, Common*

A tiny, soft, squeezable plushy.

When you squeeze this plushy as a Utilize action or a Bonus Action, it emits a squeaking sound that can be heard by creatures up to 10 feet away.

*Made by Marvin*  
---

**Plushie of Warmth**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

A tiny, soft plushy that emits warmth.

The plushy generates enough dry heat to keep the temperature within 10 feet of it at 95 degrees Fahrenheit (35 degrees Celsius).

*Made by Marvin*  
---

**Pole of Angling and Collapsing**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

While holding this 10-foot pole, you can speak a command word and transform it into a fishing pole with a hook, a line, and a reel. Speaking the command word again changes the fishing pole back into a normal 10-foot pole.

While holding this 10-foot pole, you can use an action to speak a command word and cause it to collapse into a 1-foot-long rod, for ease of storage. The pole's weight doesn't change. You can use an action to speak a different command word and cause the rod to revert to a pole; however, the rod will elongate only as far as the surrounding space allows.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Pot of Growing**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This 10-pound pot can grow any small nonmagical plant. It magically provides nutrients and water to the plant.

As a Magic action, you can touch the pot and cause a glass dome to appear over the plants growing in it. While the dome is up, the plant is treated as if it was planted in its proper environment, with the dome providing sunlight, temperature, humidity, and all other environmental factor control. You can remove the dome as a Magic action.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Potion of Air Breathing**  
*Potion, Common*

You can breathe above water for 24 hours after drinking this potion.

This potion smells and tastes like dirt.

*Made by Gavin*  
---

**Potion of Tail Growth**  
*Potion, Common*

When you drink this potion, you grow a tail.  If you already have a tail, you grow another one.

You can only have as many tails equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

*Made by Marvin*  
---

**Potion of Tail Reduction**  
*Potion, Common*

When you drink this potion, you lose a tail.

Made by Marvin  
---

**Potion of Tremorsense**  
*Potion, Common*

When you drink this potion, you have Tremorsense out to 20 feet (unless it is already higher) for 10 minutes. You must be in contact with stone, mud, or dirt to use this Tremorsense.

This potion is a grey liquid with dark particles floating within it.

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Pronoun Pin**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This round metal pin can be attached to or detached from any article of clothing as a Magic action. While the pin is worn, it displays the current pronouns of the wearer. Any creature that understands at least one language can read the pin. 

Additionally, you can cast Detect Pronouns while wearing the pin. Once you have done so, you cannot cast it again until the next dawn.

*Made by Lily*  
---

**Psychic Lance**  
*Weapon (Lance), Common*

This lance appears to be made out of shimmering glass. It leaves no physical wounds when it strikes, instead passing right through its targets. Despite this, any would-be wielders can hold it without issue.

**Ψ-Weapon.** This weapon deals Psychic damage instead of Piercing damage.

*Made by Christopher*  
---

**Quicktimer**  
*Wondrous Item, Common (Requires Attunement)*

You can use this timepiece to take a Reaction when you have already taken a Reaction before the start of your next turn. The Reaction cannot be used to cast a spell. The quicktimer cannot be used again until the next dawn.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Redsmith Crucible Set**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

This set of magic smith's tools from the dwarven Redsmith family includes a stone crucible that's been enchanted with minor transmutation magic. If you are Proficient with smith's tools or are a dwarf, you can use a Magic action to speak the tools' command word and lower a nonmagical weapon made of metal into the crucible and hold it there for 10 minutes. At the end of that time, make a DC 10 Strength check using the smith's tools. On a success, the weapon is magically reforged. A reforged weapon counts as magical, and when you pull the reforged weapon from the crucible, you can change it into another type of weapon that shares the same properties as the original one. For example, a light hammer could become a handaxe, or a longsword could become a warhammer. These benefits last for 24 hours, at which point the weapon becomes nonmagical once more and returns to its original form.

Once this property of the tools has been used, it can't be used again for 24 hours. In the meantime, the tools can still be used as magic smith's tools.

*From the Griffon's Saddlebag 2\.*  
---

**Redsmith Hammer**  
*Weapon (Light Hammer), Common*

This dwarven smithing hammer has runes running from end to end. When beaten against an anvil, the runes around the hammer glow a bright red and emanate heat, allowing a blacksmith to use the hammer to mold weapons and armor using only the hammer and anvil.

*From the Griffon's Saddlebag 2*   
---

**Rescue Ring**  
*Wondrous Item (Ring), Common, Consumable*

Whenever you start your turn underwater and you are Unconscious, the Rescue Ring causes you to rise 60 feet toward the surface. The Rescue Ring prevents you from sinking deeper while you are Unconscious. Once you regain consciousness, the Rescue Ring becomes inoperable and is consumed, unless you die before regaining consciousness.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Sacrificial Dagger**  
*Weapon (Dagger), Common*

The dagger is covered in abyssal runes, depicting commitments of evil made in service of a demon lord.

The wielder may take a Bonus Action to cut itself with the dagger, dealing 1d4 Piercing damage to itself that cannot be reduced. The wielder's Hit Point maximum is also reduced by this amount. The dagger then gains \+1 to attacks and damage for 1 minute.

Additionally, while holding this dagger, you gain a \+3 bonus to Intelligence (Religion) checks made about rituals that require a knife.

*Made by Aris, repassed from WM3*  
---

**Scary Soda**  
*Potion, Common, Consumable*

*This bubbly soda tastes like freshly picked grapefruit with a hint of lime. It's a pretty good soda, but the effects aren't pleasant.*

When you drink the soda, you become Frightened of it for one minute. A creature Frightened by the soda is inclined to scream.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Self-Replicating Truffle**  
*Wondrous Item (Common), Consumable, Exotic*

*As a Magic action while holding a Self-Replicating Truffle, you can cause a truffle (1/4lb of rations) to appear next to it. Once you use this ability, it can't be used again until the next dawn.*

You can also eat a Self-Replicating Truffle. It counts as 1 lb of rations.

*Made by Christopher*

**Shield Gauntlets**  
*Shield, Common*

Unlike a regular shield, a hand with this equipped counts as a free hand but does not passively confer a bonus to AC.

If a hand wearing these gauntlets is free, you can use a Bonus Action to gain a \+2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. You do not gain this benefit if you have another shield donned.

*Made by V*  
---

**Sinister Potion**  
*Common, Potion*

*Often forbidden to sell, this potion seems Sinister to whoever dares to lay their eyes upon it.*

When you would drink the potion, you must first make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the act is too scary for you, and you waste whatever action or Bonus Action you used to attempt to drink it. On a success, you drink the potion and you also gain its Sinister quality, gaining a \+5 bonus to Charisma (Intimidation) checks for the next minute.

*Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys*  
---

**Spellbook Jacket**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

As a Magic action, this jacket can be placed onto a spellbook. While on a spellbook, the spellbook functions as an Enduring Spellbook. As a Magic action, the creature who placed the jacket on the spellbook can remove the jacket

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Spooky Costume**  
*Wondrous Item (Costume), Common*

*With the popularity of Liar's Night continuing to grow across Toril, people have begun to create a variety of magical costumes. This costume was made to scare all who see it, depicting a vile monster or other terrifying figure.*

While wearing this costume, you can use a Magic action to cast Cause Fear (Save DC 10). Once the spell is cast, it can't be cast from the costume again until the next sunset.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Ssissuragaaaaggg's Horn**  
*Wondrous Item, Common, Exotic*

As a Magic Action you can blow the horn to summon an Abyssal Chicken in an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet. The Abyssal Chicken disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or after 1 minute.

The Abyssal Chicken is hostile to all creatures, including you. It rolls its own Initiative and acts on its own turns. It pursues and attacks the nearest creature to the best of its ability.

Once you use the horn, it can't be used until next Dawn.

*Adapted from Dungeon Crawl Classics \- Quick Start Rules & Starter Adventure. Made by Jackson*  
---

**Stinky Cheese**  
*Food and Drink, Common, Exotic*

*A delicacy to rodents. It smells quite foul, but tastes very good. It's known to cause scuffles and violent crime.*

A creature that eats these cheese must make a DC 11 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save the creature gains an uncontrollable desire to eat all stinky cheese it sees for the next hour.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Stupid Floaties**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

*Yay, floaties\!*

As a Magic action, you can inflate them. While inflated and you are wearing them, you have a Swim Speed equal to your Speed, but you cannot swim deeper underwater. If you take Piercing damage while they are inflated, they pop and they are destroyed. You can also take a Magic action to deflate the floaties.

*Made by Landon*  
---

**Undertaker's Axe**  
*Battleaxe, Greataxe, or Halberd, Common*

You cannot knock a creature out with this axe. When you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with the Undertaker's Axe, you chop off their head, or another equivalent body part, if any. 

*Made by Gavin*  
---

**Unreliable Weapon**  
*Weapon (any), Common*

When you hit with an unreliable weapon, instead of adding an ability modifier to damage rolls, you deal an extra 1d6 damage of the same type.

*Made by Gavin*  
---

**Vulture Mask**  
*Wondrous Item, Common*

While wearing this mask, you can cast Cause Fear with it (DC 10). The mask can't cast this spell again until the next dawn.  
![][image2]  
*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Weapon of The Mariner's Catch**  
*Weapon (Any, Typically Javelin or Spear), Common*

This magic weapon deals an additional 1d4 damage of its type when it is used to attack a beast with a Swim Speed. Any beast damaged in this way remains pristine and fully intact for the sake of cooking and consuming.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

## Uncommon Items {#uncommon-items}

---

**\+2 Tool**  
*Generic variant (Tool or Artisan Tool), Uncommon*

You gain a \+2 bonus to ability checks made using this tool.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Adamantine Psychic Blade Gauntlets**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

Each of these heavy leather gloves bears a violet crystal set on a small plate of adamantine.

These gloves enhance the psychic blades of Soulknifes. When you hit an object with an attack with your psychic blades, you deal force damage instead of psychic damage, and your hit is a Critical Hit.

*Inspired by Magic Item Compendium 3.5e, Made by Aris*  
---

**Adgrac Cavebraids' Steel Boots of the Unstable Mineshaft**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement by a Dwarf), Exotic*

Specially designed by Cavebraid himself. These boots are designed to help dwarven miners detect cave-ins before they occur.

While wearing these boots, you have a Tremorsense with a range of 20 feet. When you use your Stonecunning ability, your Tremorsense range increases to 60 feet. 

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Anchor**  
*Weapon (Greataxe), Uncommon (Requires Attunement)*

This greataxe is a massive anchor on a chain, and as such, deals Bludgeoning or Piercing damage instead of Slashing.

When you make an attack with it on your turn, your reach with the weapon is increased by 10 feet. When you hit a target with this weapon, you can choose to pull the target up to 10 feet towards you.

While holding this weapon, effects cannot forcibly push you or knock you Prone against your will.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Bag of Boars**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*This bag made from gray cloth appears empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals the presence of a small, pigskin object.*

You can take a Magic action to pull the pigskin object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling on the table below. See the Monster Manual for the creature's stat block. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 Hit Points.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies, and it acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. You can take a Bonus Action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, such as attacking an enemy. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature.

Once three pigskin objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag can't be used again until the next dawn.

1d8    Creature  
1    Pig (the Trade Good)  
2      Boar  
3      Boar  
4      Boar  
5      Boar  
6      Giant Boar  
7      Giant Boar  
8      Gorthok the Thunder Boar \- if the pigskin transforms into Gorthok, the bag cannot be used again for 7 days. At the end of each of Gorthok's turns while he is bloodied, you must make a DC 15 animal handling check. On a failure, Gorthok is now hostile to you and your allies and doesn't listen to your orders. He targets you in particular.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Bead of Night**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

A concentrated pearl of pure black. It feels like rippling glass.

**Fragile.** A Bead of Night has 1 hit point and 10 AC. A creature holding it can take a Utilize Action to crush it, destroying it.

**Throw.** Alternatively, as a Utilize Action, you can throw a Bead of Night to a spot you can see within 30 feet of you, destroying it.

**Darkness.** Whenever a Bead of Night is destroyed, it casts Darkness on the point that it was destroyed at (no Concentration required). When cast in this way, it lasts 1 minute instead of 10\.

*Made by Christopher*  
---

**Belt of One Mighty Blow**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

As a Bonus Action, you can activate this item, and the next time you hit with a melee weapon attack before the end of your turn, you deal extra damage determined by the type of weapon. A light weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage, a two-handed weapon deals an extra 3d6 damage, and all other attacks deal an extra 2d6 damage.

Once used, this item can't be used again until the next dawn.

*From S3 & 3.5e*

*Original item by Aris*  
---

**Bent Silver Spoon**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement by an Aberrant Sorcerer)*

*This silver spoon has strange properties that channel psychic power.* 

The spoon enhances the flavor of any soup or similarly spoonable dish by drawing out the more nuanced flavors that are often overlooked. It also tarnishes and dim when touched by a poison. If tarnished this way, the spoon remains so until the next dawn, when its normal luster magically reappears.

The spoon can be used as an Arcane Focus, and while you hold it, you gain a \+1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your Sorcerer spells and your psionic features, such as Telekinetic. 

If you are holding the spoon and you roll psychic damage, you can focus your attention on the spoon to deal maximum damage on one of the damage dice. Once this feature has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

*Adapted From TGS2 by Landon*  
---

**Blast Disk**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

As a Magic action, you can activate this blast disk and place it on an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. When you activate the blast disk, you can set the trigger to either proximity or a timer.

**Proximity.** The next creature of small size or larger that enters the space of the blast disk triggers an explosion.

**Timer.** You can set a number of rounds until the disk explodes. This number can't be less than 1 or greater than 10\. At the start of your turn after that many rounds have passed, the disk explodes.

When the disk explodes, all creatures within 10 feet of the disk must make a DC 14 Dexterity save. The creature takes 5d6 fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. This explosion also damages objects in the area, and destroys this item.

*From 3.5e and S3, Made by Aris*  
---

**Boots of Snowsense**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

While wearing these boots you have a Tremorsense of 30 feet. You must be on snow to use this Tremorsense.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Brooch of Night's Vigil**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement)*

While wearing this brooch, you gain the following benefits:

* You gain Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet if you don't already have Darkvision.  
* You have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception and Survival) checks at night.

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Cattering Ram**  
*Wondrous Item (Portable Ram), Uncommon, Unique*

*The Cattering Ram is an adamantine tube that, through the power of magic, was partially polymorphed into a Cat. It appears to be a Calico cat, having ears, whiskers, paws, a tail, and an expressive face, all while retaining its original tubular shape.*

You can receive assistance from the Cattering Ram to break down doors. When doing so, you gain a \+4 bonus and Advantage on the Strength check (This does not stack with a normal Portable Ram).

**Sentience.** The Cattering Ram is a sentient, Chaotic Neutral portable ram with an Intelligence of 3, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 7\. It has a Speed and Climb Speed of 40\. The Cattering Ram is a lazy and chaotic entity. It enjoys walking around and ramming into random objects and creatures nearby. The Cattering Ram can change its expression and create audible Cat noises as it wishes.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Cleats of Stomping**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

While you wear these boots, you can pass through the space of Prone creatures as if they were Incapacitated. When you pass through the space of a Prone creature, you can choose to deal 1d6 Piercing damage to it. A creature takes this damage no more than once per turn.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Clockwork Spurs**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*These spurs have a cog for a rowel, that rotates and makes clicking noises at timed intervals.*

While wearing these spurs and mounting a creature, you gain the following abilities:

*Perfect Command***.** As a reaction when your steed would make an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can forgo rolling to get a 10 on the die.

*Maintain Order***.** Your mount has Immunity to the charmed and frightened conditions.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Coalescence Creator**   
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon* 

*A magical bowl that shreds and mixes edible organic material placed within.* 

After placing a pound of two or more different sources of edible organic material within (to a maximum of 5 lbs.), you can spend 10 minutes to create a Smoothie with this bowl. A creature can consume this Smoothie as a Utilize Action. Consuming this Smoothie restores 1 Hit Point, and the Smoothie provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for one day. 

A Smoothie created this way loses its magical properties if it isn't consumed within 24 hours.

*Made by Marvin*  
---

**Combat Sword Sheath**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

This sword sheath is designed to be used effectively in combat even while a sword is sheathed in it.

When a sword is sheathed in this sheath, it deals Bludgeoning damage, instead of the normal damage type of the weapon. While a sword isn't sheathed in it, it can be used as a club (or greatclub, if its a greatsword sheath)

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Corroded Circuit**  
*Wondrous Item (Uncommon, Requires Attunement by a Construct), Futuristic, Unique*

A metal wafer with impossibly small magic sigils running across it. It's half-melted, rusted, and blown to bits. You aren't \*really\* gonna stick this thing in your head, right?

*Integrated***.** While you live, this item can't be removed from you against your will. However, once installed, it takes a Bastion Turn to safely unattune from it, as it is a very delicate process.

*Bludgeon Protocol***.** You gain a \+1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with Unarmed Strikes.

*Overclock***.** As a Bonus Action, you can trigger a dangerous overclock, driving your body past its limits for 1 minute. For the duration, your Unarmed Strikes deal an extra 1d6 Force damage on a hit, and your movement speed increases by 10 feet. Once used, this Bonus Action can't be used again until the next dawn.

*Directive Corruption***.** Alien instructions jab into your mind, filling your thoughts with confused, garbled messages. You have Disadvantage on Intelligence saving throws.

(Note: you cannot attune to both this and an Eldritch Claw Tattoo.)

*Made by Christopher*  
---

**Crystalline Tool**  
*Generic Variant (Tool or Artisan Tool), Uncommon, Consumable*

*This tool is sculpted out of fragile magic crystals. They are carved with very small runes that channel the magic of the crystals into the wielder allowing them to perform much better than they normally would.*

You gain a \+5 bonus to ability checks made using this tool. After making the check, if you are Proficient with the tool roll 1d6. On a 1 the tool is destroyed. If you are not Proficient with the tool, it's destroyed once.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Dream Token**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*This token contains a sliver of a psionic crystal. Most are worn as brooches, but others take the form of coins or keys.* 

When you sleep with it on your person, you remain fully lucid when you dream, and when you wake, you clearly remember everything that transpired. As an action within ten minutes of you waking, you can bind the dreams you experienced while you slept to the key, which remains bound until the next dawn. While bound, any creature who sleeps with this token on its person experiences the bound dreams instead of creating its own.

*Inspired by an item from Exploring Eberron. Made by Aris*  
---

**Dream Viewer**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

As a Magic action, you can use this lens to begin viewing the dreams of an Asleep creature within 5 feet of you (if it has any). This requires Concentration, as if it was a spell. Your Concentration also breaks if you are no longer within 5 feet of the creature or if the creature is no longer Asleep. This is considered divination magic.

*Made by Landon*  
---

**Drow House Insignia**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*Each drow house insignia is keyed to a specific noble house of a particular drow city, and is wrought to reflect the house that created it. Some might be stylized versions of crossbows, javelins, maces, or even just spiders.*

Insignias come in one of two variants: Spell or Transmitting.

**Spell.** You can use the insignia to cast a single Level 1, 2, or 3 spell determined at its creation, with the more powerful spells reserved for drow of significant station or influence. Once the insignia casts the spell, it cannot cast it again until 24 hours have passed. These insignia typically contain one of the following spells: Blur, Enhance Ability, Comprehend Languages, Feather Fall, Jump, Levitate, Shield, Spider Climb, Unseen Servant, and Water Breathing. 

**Transmitting.** As a Magic action, you can speak a command word and utter a short message of no more than 50 words or less into the insignia, which transmits it up to 1 mile away to another creature wearing an insignia of the same house. The recipient hears the message in its mind. To transmit the message, you must name the intended target as part of the command word. Should the target not be wearing its drow house insignia, the message is lost, but you have no way of knowing whether it successfully transmits or not. The insignia can transmit messages three times, and regains a use every 8 hours.

Since each insignia is slightly different—even those from the same noble house—a person aware of the unique shape of an individual insignia can magically locate that amulet with a scrying or similar spell.

**Identifying an Insignia.** A DC 15 Intelligence (History) check reveals the house to which an insignia is keyed. Drow gain a \+5 bonus on these checks.

*From 3.5e Drow of the Underdark, ported by Aris*  
---

**Dude’s Rock**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*Hell yeah, brother.*

Carved from the burning mass of a fallen star, this rock is striated with streaks of red, white, and blue. This rock has 2 charges. When you hold it, you can use a Magic action to expend a charge and choose an effect from the following list:

* Manifest a paper plate with one medium-well hamburger, eight buffalo wings, and two hot dogs in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the rock. Consuming the food as a Utilize action grants 5 Temporary Hit Points.  
* A six-pack of beer bottles appears next to the rock. The taste is identical to Anheuser-Busch Natural Light. Consuming all six beers as a Utilize action gives you Advantage against the next saving throw you make against the Frightened condition within the next hour.  
* The rock plays an otherworldly tune. The exact song is up to the discretion of the GM, but must fit under the umbrella of “Dad Rock.” You have Advantage on the next Unarmed Strike you make within the next hour.

The rock regains any expended charges at dawn.

Made by Ryan Gabrillo, Repassed from WM3  
---

**Emergency Wound Closure Express**  
*Wondrous Item (Amulet), Uncommon (Requires Attunement), Consumable*

Whenever you are reduced to 0 Hit Points and become Unconscious while wearing this amulet, you are immediately stabilized. Once the amulet is used this way, its magic is expended, and the item is consumed.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Expanding Shield**  
*Shield, Uncommon*

This shield is made of many connected gears, allowing it to expand to block off a space.

As a Magic action, you can activate the shield, doffing it and placing it onto the tile you occupy. When you do so, choose a direction (North, East, South, or West). The shield expands, transforming into a 5 foot wide and 5 foot tall blockade in the direction you chose.

*Blockade.* The blockade is a Medium object with 17 AC and 21 (6d6) Hit Points. It has Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage, and Vulnerability to Thunder damage. Nothing is able to pass through the blockade. The blockade regains all Hit Points at dawn.

The blockade is immobile, able to hold up 5,000lbs of weight. More weight causes the blockade to deactivate, transforming back into a shield. A creature can take a Utilize action to make a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check, moving the fixed rod up to 10 feet on a successful check.

When the block is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it deactivates transforming back into a shield. It is unable to be reactivated until the next dawn.

As a Magic action, the creature who activated the shield can speak its command word, deactivating the blockade transforming it back into a shield. If the creature is within 5 feet of the blockade, it can don the shield as part of the same action.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Focus Circlet**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement by a Spellcaster)*

You have a \+3 bonus to Constitution saving throws made to maintain Concentration.

*Adapted from The Griffin's Saddlebag 2 by Gem*  
---

**Fool's Scepter**  
*Weapon (Staff), Uncommon*

*The Nilbog spirit loves convincing goblins to make these funny staffs.*

As a Magic action, you can use the scepter to mock a creature within 60 feet. It must make a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw. On a fail it takes 2d4 Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on its next Attack Roll before the end of its next turn.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Frozen Memories**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement), Exotic*

*You can feel the memories of bygone beings resonating in this shard of black ice.*

While holding this shard, you can draw on the memories stored in the ice as a Magic action. Choose one skill in which you lack proficiency. For the next hour, you have proficiency in the chosen skill. Drawing upon these memories takes a toll on your own. After the hour is up, you lose proficiency in two randomly determined skills. You can only gain one proficiency in a skill at a time.

If you have less than two proficiencies in a skill, you cannot use this item. You regain all lost proficiencies after completing a Long Rest.

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Gelatin Cube**  
*Wondrous Item, Consumable, Uncommon*

*By mixing a Gelatinous cube, hot salt water, and various goops you can make a sticky, acidic, and volatile gel. Using magic to shrink a chunk of it into a 2 inch cube allows it to become stable, until it is released again.*

As a Magic action, you can activate the cube and throw it to a point up to 60 feet away. Upon landing, it expands into a 10ft Cube. If the space it is thrown is smaller than this, it expands to fill the space. The Cube is partially transparent, allowing creatures to see through it as normal while still being visible. Attacks and spells can be made through the Cube as if it wasn't there. The area covered by the Cube is difficult terrain. The Cube evaporates after 1 minute.

When it expands, every creature in the area of the Cube must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed saving throw, the creature takes 7 (2d6) Acid damage, and becomes engulfed by the Cube. On a successful one, the creature takes no damage, but is moved to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of itself that is not inside the Cube. If there is no unoccupied space available or the creature chooses to not move, it suffers the effects of failing the saving throw.

When a creature enters the Cube's area or ends its turn there, it must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 7 (2d6) Acid damage and becoming engulfed on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature can only take this damage once per turn.

**Engulfed*.*** Any creature engulfed by the Cube is suffocating, can't cast spells with a Verbal component, and has the Restrained condition. An engulfed creature can try to escape by taking an action to make a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the creature escapes and enters the nearest unoccupied space, no longer being engulfed.

As an action, a creature within 5 feet of the Cube can attempt to pull out an engulfed creature by succeeding on a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check, moving the creature to the nearest unoccupied space on a success, causing it to no longer be engulfed. The puller takes 7 (2d6) Acid damage whether or not it succeeds on the check.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Gun Buddy**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement)*

*A little goober who hangs from a weapon to manage your ammunition.*

As a Magic action you can attach a Gun Buddy to any weapon with the Ammunition property. While attached, you can ignore the Loading property of the weapon and you don't require a free hand to draw or load ammo into the weapon.

**Sentience.** A Gun Buddy is a sentient Unaligned object with an Intelligence of 6, a Wisdom of 6, and a Charisma of 14\. It has hearing and Blindsight out to 5 feet (it is blind beyond). A Gun Buddy loves nothing more than guns and ammunition, and will always try to grab some if it is in reach and load it into the nearest weapon. Other than that they have fun by swinging around.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Harpoon of Reeling**  
*Weapon (Javelin), Uncommon*

This magic javelin is attached by a rope, and immediately after the attack, the weapon flies back into your hand.

If you possess the weapon mastery feature and have selected the Javelin as one of your masteries, this javelin has the unique 'Pull' weapon mastery.

*Pull.* If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can pull the creature up to 10 feet straight towards yourself if it is Large or smaller.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Holy Hand Grenade**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable, Exotic*

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with lighting the Holy Hand Grenade and throwing it to a point up to 60 feet away. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d6 Radiant Damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

*Inspired by Terraria, Made by Gem*  
---

**Horn of Plenty**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Exotic*

*This horn is full of delicious food and celebratory gifts. Anyone would be grateful to have a chance to take from it*

This horn has 3 charges, which it regains daily at dawn. 

As a Bonus Action, you can expend a charge to eat from the horn, or feed food from it to another creature within 5ft of yourself. The creature that eats from the horn regains 3d4+3 Hit Points.

As a Magic action, you can expend all 3 charges to cast Goodberry.

*Inspired by Terraria, Made by Gem*  
---

**Horns of Taurus**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement).*

These massive black horns are incredibly difficult to hold on your head, but imbue you with a building rage.

**Bulky.** Your speed is reduced by 10 feet.

**Building Rage.** While in initiative, your speed increases by 15 feet at the end of each of your turns, up to a maximum of 60 movement speed. Your speed resets if you are Incapacitated or you leave Initiative.

**Raging Charge.** At the start of your turn, if you have 60 feet of movement speed, you gain the following until the end of your next turn:

\- You cannot cast spells, concentrate on spells, or make ranged attacks.  
\- The first melee attack you hit as part of an Attack action deals an extra 1d8 damage of the weapon's damage type.

Once this effect triggers, it cannot be triggered until you roll Initiative again.

*Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.*  
*By Miel*  
---

**Jester Hat**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement)*

While wearing this hat, you can use a Magic action to cast Tasha's Hideous Laughter (Save DC 13). It can be used twice, regaining all uses daily at dawn.

Whenever you make a Charisma (Performance) check, you can add 1d4 to the roll.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Lil' Locksmith**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*I'm on it, boss\!*

The Lil' Locksmith is sentient and has the personality of a locksmith artisan. Its alignment is determined by rolling on the alignment table in the "Sentient Magic Items" section in the Dungeon Master's Guide (or determined by the GM granting this item). Regardless of its alignment, the Lil' Locksmith uses the 'Commoner' stablock, with the exception that the Lil' Locksmith is of the tiny size category, and has Expertise in thieves' tools. The Lil' Locksmith has its own goals and motivations, and might require an incentive to take orders from a wielder, such as payment, a task, or some other form of compensation.

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Mak’Arrel’s Riffmaker**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement)*

*Riff*. You can take a Magic action to strum the cittern and cause an illusory riff to emanate from it.

*Cacophony*. This cittern has 4 charges and regains 1d4 \+ 1 charges daily at dawn. While wielding this cittern, you can cast a spell from it. A spell cast from the cittern has a save DC of 13\. Choose the spell from the following list: Shatter (2 charges), Thunderwave (1 charge).

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Mystery Potion (Uncommon)**  
*Potion, Uncommon, Consumable*

*Typically made as a byproduct by alchemists, this potion is fully unpredictable. Swirling this liquid causes its color to change rapidly between a variety of vibrant colors. Its smell is purely alchemical, providing no insight as to what could be inside.*

When you drink this potion, roll on the table below, gaining the effects of drinking or applying the rolled potion.

A creature can spend 1 hour studying the potion to attempt to identify and stabilize its effects. After the hour is done, the creature must make a DC 15 Intelligence (Alchemist's Supplies) check. On a pass, roll on the table transforming the Mystery Potion into the rolled potion.

\`\`\`  
1d100 | Potion Effect  
1-3   | Potion of Climbing  
4-6   | Potion of Comprehension  
7-9   | Potion of Healing  
10-12 | Potion of Watchful Rest  
13-15 | Bottled Breath  
16-18 | Oil of Slipperiness  
19-21 | Philter of Adoration  
22-24 | Potion of Acid Resistance  
25-27 | Potion of Advantage  
28-30 | Potion of Animal Friendship  
31-33 | Potion of Cold Resistance  
34-36 | Potion of Fire Breath  
37-39 | Potion of Fire Resistance  
40-42 | Potion of Force Resistance  
43-45 | Potion of Greater Healing  
46-48 | Potion of Growth  
49-51 | Potion of Hill Giant Strength  
52-54 | Potion of Lightning Resistance  
55-57 | Potion of Necrotic Resistance  
58-60 | Potion of Poison  
61-63 | Potion of Poison Resistance  
64-66 | Potion of Polychromy  
67-69 | Potion of Psionic Fortitude  
70-72 | Potion of Psychic Resistance  
73-75 | Potion of Pugilism  
76-78 | Potion of Radiant Resistance  
79-81 | Potion of Thunder Resistance  
82-84 | Potion of Water Breathing  
85-00 | Roll Again  
\`\`\`

*Made by Gem*  
---

**NR-G Sports Soda**  
*Potion, Uncommon, Consumable*

*“A quick toxicology test on some of the victims \[of this drink\] has come back positive for um... all kinds of stuff, really. Methadone, phencyclidine, tetrahydrocannabinol, a variety of opiates, benzodiazepines, barbiturates, amphetamines... the list just goes on and on and on.”*

When you drink this potion, your Speed increases by 10 feet for 1 hour.

*From Risk of Rain 2, Made by Althaea*  
---

**Pendant of Bloody Gust**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement), Consumable*

*"May your rage bring haste.”*

A blood agate in the shape of a curling mote of wind, fashioned into a necklace or ring. It reacts to the presence of its holder's blood.

While you are Bloodied, you gain 10 additional movement speed.

Additionally, as a Magic action while you are bloodied, or as a Reaction to gaining the bloodied condition, you can cast Haste using this stone, targeting yourself. When you do so, the stone becomes nonmagical.

*Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance*  
*Made by Miel*  
---

**Poofy Snowball**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

As a Magic action you can cast Snow Cloud (Save DC 13\) by throwing the ball at a place within 60 feet.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Pointe Stilettos**  
*Weapon (Rapier), Uncommon (Requires Attunement)*

You have a \+1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with these weapons.

These two rapiers transform into boots when you put them on your feet as a Utilize action. While they are boots, each can be wielded as a rapier while on a foot. You can transform them back into a more normal rapier form as Utilize action.

They also have the Thrown (20/60) property in either form, and don't require a hand to be thrown while they're boots. In either form, they magically return at the end of your turn if what was used to throw them is free, whether that's to your hand or your feet. When they do so, you must succeed on a DC 5 Dexterity Saving Throw or take 1d4 Piercing damage from them.

*Original Item by Aris*   
---

**Rat King's Crown**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement), Unique*

*This crown is made of matted fur from many different rats. Rats view those who wear the crown as powerful, but not a voice of authority.*

While wearing this crown, Hostile rodents become Indifferent to you. If you deal damage to one of these creatures, this effect is suppressed for 1 minute.

**Rodent Influence.** As a Magic action while wearing the crown, you can cast Dominate Beast (Save DC 13), targeting a rodent that is not Hostile to you. When cast this way, the spell lasts up to 1 hour, but once the spell ends an affected creature becomes immune to it for 24 hours.

**Conjure *RATS*.** As a Magic action you summon a Swarm of Rats at a point within 60 feet of yourself. It is Indifferent to you and your allies. This Swarm of Rats disappears after a day or once it drops to 0 Hit Points. You cannot use this ability until the previous Swarm of Rats created by it disappears.

**Cursed.** Your teeth and hair begin to grow rapidly. Each day you must spend 1 hour gnawing on an object to prevent your teeth from exceeding their natural length. If you let your teeth grow unimpeded for 3 days in a row, you transform into a Giant Rat as if through polymorph. This form lasts until you have no Temporary Hit Points left, at which point you die and revert form. You can't remove the crown or end your attunement unless you have done an hour of gnawing within the last 24 hours.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Scallywag's Sash**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*This harness, belt, or sash holds up to four flintlock pistol holsters, making for easy swapping between loaded firearms in a fight.*

While wearing the Scallywag's Sash, you may stow up to four pistols in its holsters. When you take the attack action on your turn, you may draw a pistol from the Sash before you make an attack, and stow it in the sash once the attack resolves, independently from the Equipping and Unequipping Weapons part of your attack. This benefit only applies to flintlock (Renaissance) pistols. 

*Made by Patrick*  
---

**Second Edition Crowbar**  
*Weapon (Warhammer), Uncommon*

*“Crowbar/prybar/wrecking bar allows for both prying and smashing\! Carbon steel, so it should last for a very long time, at least until the 3rd edition arrives.”*

You have a \+1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you hit a creature with Hit Points equal to its Hit Point maximum, this weapon deals an additional 1d8 damage.

You can use this as a Crowbar.

*From Risk of Rain, Made by Althaea*  
---

**Shawl of the Reaper**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement), Exotic*

*Reaper's Training*. When attuned to this shawl, you have Proficiency with Sickles and Glaives.

*Strength from Harvest*. When you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with a Sickle or Glaive, you gain 2d4 Temporary Hit Points.

*Blessing of Myrkul*. When you succeed on a saving throw against a spell from a spellcaster, the spellcaster takes Necrotic damage equal to twice the spell's level.

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Shield of Blinding**   
*Shield, Uncommon* 

This shield has three charges. When a creature within 5 feet of you misses you with a melee weapon attack, you may use your reaction to expend one charge of the shield. That creature must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be Blinded until the end of its next turn. This shield regains 1d3 charges daily at dawn.

*Made by Chrys*  
---

**Slugger**  
*Weapon (Greatclub), Uncommon*

When you score a Critical hit with this weapon, you can choose to summon a Giant Slug within 5ft of yourself. It uses the Giant Snail statblock, however it lacks the Shell Defense action.

The Giant Slug is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the Giant Slug shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger. The Giant Slug disappears when it takes damage or at the start of your next turn.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Sporophore**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

As a Magic action, you can use this sporophore to spread spores in 5 foot square on a surface within 5 feet of you. The spores are of a fungi of your choice, and after 10 minutes, it fully sprouts, provided the area is in dim light or darkness for the entire duration. The fungus can be one of the following:

* Brown Mold,  
* Yellow Mold,  
* Russet Mold,  
* Violet Fungus,  
* Shrieker Fungus,  
* Gas Spore Fungus,  
* Myconid Sprout,  
* Campestri,  
* Jammer Leech,  
* An Edible Fungi,  
* An Exotic Fungi,

A sporophore only contains enough spores for one fungi. It regains its spores daily at dusk.

*Made by Aris*  
---

**Stratagem Ball: OPS**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

Stratagem Balls are handheld beacons that can be thrown to call down various forms of support. This one has been modified for long-range transmit, allowing use outside of SEAF operations. As a Bonus Action, you can throw a Stratagem Ball up to 60 feet away. Stratagem Balls can only be used outdoors.

After throwing the Ball, an artillery shell lands at the same spot at the start of your next turn. Any creature in a 5-foot Emanation must make a DC 13 Dexterity save or take 6d6 fire damage, or half on a success.

*Made by Lily*  
---

**Stratagem Ball: ESR**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

Stratagem Balls are handheld beacons that can be thrown to call down various forms of support. This one has been modified for long-range transmit, allowing use outside of SEAF operations. As a Bonus Action, you can throw a Stratagem Ball up to 60 feet away. Stratagem Balls can only be used outdoors.

After throwing the Ball, a strafing run lands at the end of your turn. Any creature in a 5-foot wide, 20-foot long line starting from the ball and pointing away from you must make a DC 13 Dexterity save or take 4d4 Piercing damage, or half on a success.

*Made by Lily*  
---

**Stratagem Ball: ENS**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

Stratagem Balls are handheld beacons that can be thrown to call down various forms of support. This one has been modified for long-range transmit, allowing use outside of SEAF operations. As a Bonus Action, you can throw a Stratagem Ball up to 60 feet away. Stratagem Balls can only be used outdoors.

After throwing the Ball, a napalm strike lands at the start of your next turn. Rr napalm lands in a 5-foot wide, 30-foot long line originating from the ball in a direction of your choice. Any creature in the napalm when it lands must make a DC 13 Dex save or take 4d8 fire damage, or half on a success. The napalm remains for one minute, and any creature that enters the napalm or starts its turn there takes 1d8 Fire damage.

*Made by Lily*  
---

**Tactical Rock Pouch**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement)*

*Many underestimate the power of having a good rock between you and the mouth of a dragon.*

Whenever you roll Initiative and are not surprised, you can choose to deploy 2d4 tactical rocks from the pouch. You throw each of these to an unoccupied space within 120 feet of yourself, where they expand becoming Medium sized boulders (Shown below). The boulders disappear after 10 minutes.

**Boulder**  
*Medium Object*

Armor Class: 17  
Hit Points: 50  
Damage Immunities: Poison, Psychic

Each boulder fills a 5ft Cube, and is large enough to provide up to Three-Quarters Cover to a standing Medium sized creature.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Tall Hat**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*These hats are almost always found in their smallest form, but through bloodlust and the love of the game, you can achieve hat perfection\!*

This hat starts being 5 inches tall. Every time you kill a creature with an Intelligence of 2 or higher while wearing this hat, it grows 1/2 inch taller. As the hat grows taller, it gains new effects, retaining the effects of all heights smaller than it. 

**5 inches:** *Your hat is of normal size.* Creatures who view your hat think that it is a normal thing to wear. Creatures who immune to the charmed condition or mind altering effects are allowed to form their own opinions. Because of the heft of the hat, you have \-1d4 to all Initiative rolls.

**10 inches:** *Your hat is starting to become quite tall.* You have \-1 to all stealth checks you make, unless you are invisible or otherwise fully hidden. As a Magic action you can tip your hat at a creature within 30ft of yourself. The target must make a DC 5 Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed by you until the start of your next turn. Once you use this ability, you can't until the next dawn.

**15 inches:** *This is a massive hat\!* Whenever a creature rolls exactly 1 above your AC, your hat gets hit causing the attacker to take notice of its elegance. When your hat is hit, the attacking creature must make a DC 5 Charisma saving throw or be Charmed by you until the end of your next turn. A creature that passes this save is immune to it for 24 hours.

**24 inches:** *Your hat is comically large.* As a Magic action you can pull your hat down onto yourself (Even if you are larger then it) causing you to go inside of it for a moment. While inside of the hat, you are considered to have half cover. Once you are hit, you are forced out of your hat (yet it remains on your head). You can only take this Magic action once per day.

**36 inches:**  *At 3 feet tall, your hat is too much to handle.* Whenever you fail an Acrobatics check by 5 or more, your hat falls off of your head.

**48 inches** *Four feet\!* Your hat catches in the wind, causing it to become even more nimble. You can take any of the previous Magic actions as a Bonus Action.

**60 inches** *FIVE WHOLE FEET\!* When you enter a space that a creature one size larger of you can't fit into (or would have to squeeze into), you hat is knocked off of your head. You gain \-1d4 to any check involving fitting into a small space.

**120 inches** *TEN FEET\!\!\!\!\!\!\!\!* By reaching ten feet, you have achieved perfection in your hat causing it to turn gold. When you put your hat on the ground, you can climb it as if it were a 10ft tall ladder, or use it as a 10ft pole. Selling a hat that is golden magically nets you an extra 500gp no matter what price you sold it for, however, the hat permanently loses its golden color.

**25,000 miles** *Your hat is now large enough to stretch across all of Toril* As a Magic action while wearing the hat, you can launch yourself through it, teleporting yourself anywhere on the same planet, as if cast by the Teleport spell. Once you use this ability you can't use it again until the next dawn.

**Curse:** Whenever this hat is knocked off of your head, you take 1d4 psychic damage and must make a DC 10 Wisdom saving through or be charmed by the hat. On a fail, you must spend your next turn trying to pick up the hat.

Made by Gem, Repassed from WM3  
---

**The Belt**  
*Weapon (whip), Unique, Uncommon*

*"Speed up\!"*

A manifestation of a scared mind, this leather belt is exceptionally long. 

You can take a Magic action to crack the belt. When you do so, creatures of your choice within 30 feet must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or become Frightened by you for 1 minute. Creatures that can't hear automatically pass the save.

Creatures that are Frightened in this way have their speed increased by 10 feet. Frightened creatures can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the effect on a success. 

Once you use this ability, it can only be used again after an hour.

*Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.*  
*Repassed From S3, by Miel.*  
---

**Throwing Dynamite**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with lighting the Throwing Dynamite and throwing it to a point up to 60 feet away. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4d6 Fire Damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Trap Powder**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon, Consumable*

*This bag is full of magical dust. When released, the dust explodes quickly covering every nearby object and sneaking its way into traps.*

As a Magic action, you can open the bag to cast Find Traps. When cast in this way, it has a range of Self and only senses traps within 60 feet of the caster. The dust glows red for a moment if it detects a trap, filling the space with Dim Light until the end of your turn. The dust magically disappears at the end of your turn.

*Made by Gem*  
---

**Umbral Hood**  
*Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement), Futuristic, Unique*

A black, silky cloak. Thin etchings are traced along its fabric, leading to a metallic device at the cloak's heart.

**Shadow Circuit.** As a Magic Action, you can activate the cloak, causing its fabric to bleed blackness. You gain the following benefits for 1 hour:
